content.problemStatement:
Chef is very happy today because he got an offer from Gordon Ramsay himself to work for him in London. Chef wants to prepare a delicious cake for Gordon to impress him, but he does not know that Gordon Ramsay is not easy to impress.

Gordon cut Chef's cake into NNN small slices (numbered 111 through NNN), placed in a row in such a way that for each valid iii, slices iii and i+1i+1i+1 are adjacent. Note that slices NNN and 111 are **not** adjacent. For each valid iii, the iii-th slice has taste AiA_iAi‚Äč. Gordon wants to put special toppings on some slices and then ask Chef to group all slices into KKK clusters in such a way that the slices in each cluster form a contiguous sequence and the sweetness of the cake is maximum possible.

For each valid iii, let's denote the topping on the iii-th slice by tit_iti‚Äč (ti=0t_i = 0ti‚Äč=0 if there is no topping on this slice); the sweetness of this slice is ti‚čÖAit_i \cdot A_iti‚Äč‚čÖAi‚Äč, so the sweetness of a slice without any special topping is 000.

The sweetness of a cluster is the total (summed up) sweetness of all slices in this cluster. Let's denote the sweetnesses of all clusters by S1,S2,‚Ä¶,SKS_1, S_2, \ldots, S_KS1‚Äč,S2‚Äč,‚Ä¶,SK‚Äč. The sweetness of the whole cake is computed as S1‚ąóS2‚ąó‚Ä¶‚ąóSKS_1 * S_2 * \ldots * S_KS1‚Äč‚ąóS2‚Äč‚ąó‚Ä¶‚ąóSK‚Äč, where the operation ‚ąó*‚ąó is defined in the following way: for any non-negative integers xxx and yyy such that x‚Č•yx \ge yx‚Č•y,
x‚ąóy=y‚ąóx=‚ąĎn=0‚Ćälog‚Ā°2(x)‚Ćč2n(‚Ćäx2n‚Ćč‚Äämod‚Ää2)(‚Ćäy2n‚Ćč‚Äämod‚Ää2)‚ÄČ.x * y = y * x = \sum_{n=0}^{\lfloor\log_2(x)\rfloor} 2^n\left(\left\lfloor\frac{x}{2^n}\right\rfloor \bmod 2\right)\left(\left\lfloor\frac{y}{2^n}\right\rfloor \bmod 2\right) \,.x‚ąóy=y‚ąóx=‚ąĎn=0‚Ćälog2‚Äč(x)‚Ćč‚Äč2n(‚Ćä2nx‚Äč‚Ćčmod2)(‚Ćä2ny‚Äč‚Ćčmod2).
It can be proven that this operation is associative, i.e. (x‚ąóy)‚ąóz=x‚ąó(y‚ąóz)(x * y) * z = x * (y * z)(x‚ąóy)‚ąóz=x‚ąó(y‚ąóz).

You should answer QQQ queries. In each query, you are given the toppings on all slices and the number of clusters KKK. Tell Chef the maximum possible sweetness of the cake!
constraints: in each query, the number of slices with toppings does not exceed 505050
content.inputFormat:
The first line of the input contains a single integer NNN.
The second line contains NNN space-separated integers A1,A2,‚Ä¶,ANA_1, A_2, \ldots, A_NA1‚Äč,A2‚Äč,‚Ä¶,AN‚Äč.
The next line contains a single integer QQQ.
For each query, two lines follow. The first of these lines contains a single integer KKK. The second line contains NNN space-separated integers t1,t2,‚Ä¶,tNt_1, t_2, \ldots, t_Nt1‚Äč,t2‚Äč,‚Ä¶,tN‚Äč.
content.outputFormat: Print a single line containing one integer ‚ÄĒ the maximum sweetness.

sampleTestCases.explanation:
Explanation:
For the first query, Chef can choose clusters in the following way: cluster 111 contains slices 111 and 222, cluster 222 contains slices 333 through 777 and cluster 333 contains slices 888 through 101010. The sweetness of the cake is (15‚čÖ2+5‚čÖ3)‚ąó(26‚čÖ1+4‚čÖ4+3‚čÖ7+13‚čÖ1+5‚čÖ5)‚ąó(5‚čÖ1+27‚čÖ1+11‚čÖ1)=33(15 \cdot 2 + 5 \cdot 3) * (26 \cdot 1 + 4 \cdot 4 + 3 \cdot 7 + 13 \cdot 1 + 5 \cdot 5) * (5 \cdot 1 + 27 \cdot 1 + 11 \cdot 1) = 33(15‚čÖ2+5‚čÖ3)‚ąó(26‚čÖ1+4‚čÖ4+3‚čÖ7+13‚čÖ1+5‚čÖ5)‚ąó(5‚čÖ1+27‚čÖ1+11‚čÖ1)=33.
